mechanism_narrative: Sustained high growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong underlying economic momentum, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold of 5%, higher borrowing costs compress corporate margins and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 5%, the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly lower than the average return when FEDFUNDS is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a sharp contraction in consumer health, which precedes widespread earnings downgrades and causes negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansionary regime, a very low unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market, increasing investor fears of wage-push inflation and subsequent aggressive monetary tightening, which is negative for equities.
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of UNRATE (when below 5%) and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a robust manufacturing sector and strong business investment, signaling broad economic strength that supports higher equity valuations.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of INDPRO's 3-month change for predicting 20-day forward returns fails to be positive and statistically significant over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated while the central bank maintains a high policy rate (FEDFUNDS), this interaction creates a stagflationary environment that severely compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: During periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day S&P 500 forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broad-based slowdown in consumer spending and construction, creating a rapid negative sentiment shock for equity markets.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly value falls by >10% month-over-month
failure_condition: Following a >10% MoM drop in HOUST, the S&P 500's 5-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is low (UNRATE < 5%) but payroll growth momentum (PAYEMS) turns negative, this interaction signals the peak of the business cycle. Markets price in an imminent recession, causing a sharp decline in equities.
failure_condition: The joint condition of low unemployment and negative payroll momentum is not followed by a statistically significant negative average 60-day return compared to all other periods.
